title: Temperatures exceed 44°C in Prayagraj and Amravati: Schools shut in 4 Odisha districts, MNREGA working hour... - Bhaskar English

full_text:
Prayagraj, Amravati record temperatures above 44°C:Schools shut in four Odisha districts; one dies of heatstroke in Kerala
- Copy link
The impact of the ongoing heatwave is intensifying across several parts of India, with temperatures soaring beyond 44°C in multiple regions. On Thursday, the highest temperature of 44.5°C was recorded in Sri Ganganagar, Rajasthan.
Closely following were Prayagraj in Uttar Pradesh and Amravati in Maharashtra, both registering 44.4°C, while Akola recorded 44.3°C.
Changes to school and work timings in Rajasthan
In response to the rising temperatures, authorities in Rajasthan have revised school timings in several districts, including Jaipur, Kota, Chittorgarh, Jaisalmer and Dausa. Schools will now commence earlier, at 7:30 AM, to minimise exposure to extreme heat.
Additionally, working hours under the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MNREGA) have been adjusted in Bikaner, Jhunjhunu, and Jaisalmer. Labourers will now work from 6:00 AM to 1:00 PM to avoid peak afternoon heat.
Meanwhile, in Kerala, one person has died due to heatstroke. This is the first reported heat-related fatality in Kannur district amid rising temperatures.
Odisha continues to reel under severe heat
The heatwave persists in Odisha, where Jharsuguda and Talcher recorded maximum temperatures of 44°C on Thursday. Across the state, 24 locations reported temperatures at or above 40°C.
Educational institutions have also been affected. In Cuttack, school and college holidays have been extended until 27 April. Meanwhile, schools in Rayagada, Jharsuguda and Ganjam districts will remain closed until 26 April due to the extreme weather conditions.
Weather forecast for the next 2 days
25 April
Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in parts of Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra.
Hot and humid conditions are expected in Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, Gujarat, Odisha and Tamil Nadu.
26 April
Heatwave conditions are forecast to continue in Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh.
There is also a likelihood of warm night conditions in parts of Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Odisha and Uttar Pradesh.
Meanwhile, very hot and humid weather is expected in Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, West Bengal, Gujarat, Goa, Odisha and Tamil Nadu.

title: Cuttack schools, colleges, and anganwadi centres to remain closed due to heatwave - prameyanews.com
search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
full_text:
Cuttack, Ap 24: In response to the intensifying heatwave, the Cuttack district administration has ordered the closure of all government and private schools, as well as Anganwadi centres, on April 24, 25, and 27.
According to District Collector Dattatraya Bhausaheb Shinde, teachers are required to attend schools and may conduct online classes to ensure the academic syllabus is completed on time. The order also applies to government, aided, and private colleges, though previously scheduled examinations will proceed as planned.
During this period, Anganwadi centres have been instructed to distribute dry rations at home rather than provide cooked meals.
Earlier, schools were allowed to operate on revised morning hours from 6:30 AM to 9:00 AM, before being shut on April 20 due to rising temperatures. Following a review of worsening conditions, the administration decided to extend the closure.
Officials have been directed to continue census-related work while adhering to heatwave safety protocols.
